The greatest integer in a real number \(x\), denoted by \([x]\), is the largest integer less than or equal to \(x\). Examples: \([5/2]=2\), \([-5/2]=-3\), \([2]=2\).

The greatest integer function is also known as the floor function, with notation \(\lfloor x \rfloor\).

The ceiling function of a real number \(x\), denoted by \(\lceil x \rceil\), is the smallest integer greater than or equal to \(x\).

  • Examples: \(\lceil 5/2 \rceil = 3\).
Prove that:$$ n \in \mathb{Z} \implies \lfloor x + n \rfloor = \lfloor x \rfloor + n $$
What is the value of \(\lfloor x \rfloor + \lfloor -x \rfloor\) when \(x\) is a real number?
0 if \(x\) is an integer and -1 otherwise.
Prove that \([x + 1/2]\) is the integer nearest to \(x\).
Prove that \([(2+\sqrt{3})^n]\) is odd whenever \(n\) is a nonnegative integer.

Consider the binomial theorem:$$ \binom{n}{k} (2+\sqrt{3})^n = 2^{n-k} + (\sqrt{3})^k$$Separate the sum into an even and odd part:$$ X + Y\sqrt{3} $$\(X\) contains the sum of all the terms where \(k\) is even. Here, \(\sqrt{3}^k\) would be an integer. As for \(Y\sqrt{3}\), that contains the sum of all the terms with odd \(k\).

Now consider \((2-\sqrt{3})^n\)$$ (2-\sqrt{3})^n = A+B\sqrt{3} $$\(A\) contains all the terms with even power. Since \((-\sqrt{3})^h\) is even when \(h\) is even, then \(A=X\).

Since \((-\sqrt{3})^h = -(\sqrt{3})^h\) when \(h\) is odd, then \(B=-Y\). This means:$$ (2-\sqrt{3})^n = X-Y\sqrt{3} $$Adding them together:$$ (2+\sqrt{3})^n + (2-\sqrt{3})^n = 2X $$This shows that \((2+\sqrt{3})^n + (2-\sqrt{3})^n\) is even. Let \(2X=E\):$$ (2+\sqrt{3})^n = E - (2-\sqrt{3})^n\) $$. Since \(0 \lt 2-\sqrt{3} \lt 1\), then \(0 \lt (2-\sqrt{3})^n \lt 1\). This means $$ E - (2-\sqrt{3})^n = O + D $$where \(O\) is an odd integer and \(D\) is a positive number less than 1. This also means:$$ [(2+\sqrt{3})^n] = [O+D] = O $$This shows that [(2+\sqrt{3})^n] is odd.
